Q. how do the priest take the chalice ? A. the priest have meditate a while , on the holy sacrament now receive , go forward to accomplish the holy sacrifice , kneel down to adore the blood of our lord , and take the chalice with prayer suitable , in the same manner as he do with the holy host , he sign he with it , import as much , as if he shall say , the blood of our lord Jesus Christ , which be the fountain and laver of our sanctification , the price of our redemption , and reparation , shed on the cross , keep my soul in security against all my enemy , and bring i to life everlasting ; while he be do this , we may say , hail sacred blood for we still flow , to show th• price of our redemption ; wash we from our sin always abound , and cleanse we by Christ bitter passion . Q. why do the priest after this , take wine ? A. such be the reverence which the church bear to this holy sacrament , that she ordain this take of wine after the communion of the chalice , lest any drop shall remain in the chalice : as also to cleanse it after the holy species . Q. why do the priest go to the corner of the altar ? A. as he take wine to cleanse the chalice , so now out of reverence to the altar , where our saviour be before offer , he go to the end of the altar , there to wash the top of his finger which have touch the bless sacrament , lest any remnant or particle of the host shall remain on they : and also because it be unbeseeming that those finger shall touch any other thing before they be wash , so that this ceremony be rather for decency than mystery . Q. but why do he do it with wine and water ? A. there be no necessity of both , either may suffice ; but the church use both , and not without mystery ; for the priest have receive the holy eucharist , be to be wash with water and wine , that be , with spiritual exultation and joy signify by the wine , and compunction of our saviour passion signify by the water ; for this sacrifice have a mixture of joy and sorrow , of joy for the holy eucharist ; of sorrow in regard of the passion represent therein ; of joy for the come of our saviour in so merciful , profitable and most love manner ; of sorrow for our sin , which have be the cause of Christ so great torment , dolour and death on the cross . as therefore both those mystery be represent in the mass , and both concur to our salvation ; so the join of wine and water in this action , signify the affection of the mind shall correspond to what they signify .

IV. of the extent of hell pain . when i consider righteous Job on the dunghill , Greg. mor . the holy Baptist hunger in the wilderness , saint James behead , saint Peter crucify , the torment and death of innumerable martyr , the manifold affliction of the holy and elect people of God , i can but consider and know assure that very great and many shall be the torment of the wicked in the world to come : since God suffer those who he dear love to be so much afflict in this life . the pain of hell in the extent of they do herein differ from all present bodily pain : that these be partial only in some particular part , joint and member of the body , while other part be free from pain : but in hell the whole man in all the sense internal and external , in all the part of his body and power of his soul , yea the most spiritual faculty shall be torment with fire and brimstone , rage and despite , grief and anguish , misery and malediction . the pain of hell be a concourse of all kind of pain , and of all at the same time , and of all of they for ever . the taste shall be punish with bitterness , the appetite with hunger , and the tongue with thirst , the sight with horror , the hear with astonishment , the smell with stench , the heart with anguish , the imagination with fear , the reason with madness , the judgement with confusion , and in the very bowel fire unquenchable . and this be most just , that as the wicked have employ all the power and part both of soul and body , as weapon of unrighteousness unto sin ; the four general meditation , of heaven . 1. the land of Canaan be promise to Abraham the father of the faithful in these word of command : Gen. 13.14 lift up now thy eye , and look from the place where thou be , northward and southward , eastward and westward — for all the land which thou see , to thou will i give it , and to thy seed for ever . the celestial Canaan be herein promise unto thou if a true son of the faithful Abraham : lift up then thy eye , oh my soul , and contemplate the ravish felicity of this land of promise . this be thy native country , where thou be at first frame by the hand of the almighty after his own image ; why then do thou not pant and breathe and sigh and long to be at home ? why be thou so well please with a strange land , and delight rather to be a pilgrim in the valley of tear , than a free denizon in the land of peace ? a foreigner in the city of Babylon than a citizen in the heavenly Jerusalem ? away , away from Lebanon , oh my soul , thy belove call thou away from the den of lion , can . 4.8 . and the mountain of the leopard : Ps. 55.6 . o that i have wing like a dove , for then will i flee away and be at rest . 2. we read that the lord bring Moses to the top of mount Pisgah , and show he the promise land ; where have the wilderness behind he , and the happy of all land before he , he represent the devout and heavenly mind soul , who consider the manifold trouble in the wilderness of this world , and the joy and felicity of the world to come , be high ravish in the contemplation and desire of the one to the extreme contempt of the other . 3. the happiness of heaven be the end of all holiness upon earth , and that must needs be the great good , which be the end of all that be good , for the end be more noble than the mean . it|be the last good we hope for , and so the most perfect , as be the perfection and accomplishment of all the good we can imagine or desire ; nay , it|be a blessedness beyond our frail imagination to comprehend : as it be write , eye have not see , nor ear hear , neither have enter into the heart of man , the thing which God have prepare for they that love he . quod deus praeparavit diligentibus se , fide non capitur , spe non attingitur , charitate non comprehenditur , desideria and vota transgreditur : acquiri potest , aestimari non potest . Aug. 1 Cor. 2.9 . 4. raise up thy affection ( oh my soul ) as to be ravish with the love of heaven , so to lament with tear of sorrow and shame thy sloth and negligence , thy coldness and indevotion , thy sinful security and earthly mindedness , and what obstruct thy way , and slacken thy pace towards this place of joy unspeakable and glorious . O how slight and trivial , how inconsiderable be all the most strict and rigid labour of repentance and mortification , of the most profound piety , and ample charity , in respect of those celestial joy whereunto they lead thou ! and if it shall once please God through the merit of Christ to receive thou into heaven , thou will then think all thy prayer and tear , sigh and groan , fast and watch , all thy labour of love both to God and man , very well spend , that they have waft thou over the troublesome wave of this world vast sea , into the haven of eternal peace and felicity . meditate . ii of the place we call heaven , and first , its greatness . that empyreal heaven , 2 Cor. 12.2 . Dent . 10.14 . 2 Chr 6.18 . which be the seat of God and of all his holy angel and saint for ever , be call also the three heaven , and the high heaven , and the heaven of heaven ; and it|be off all the place of the universe the most spacious , large and ample : for it contain all the heaven , and the earth also , even the whole creation within its verge and compass . do but consider the vastness of the sun , and of the moon , with the multitude and magnitude of all the light of heaven , most of which be great , and some of they far big than the whole body of the earth : consider also that beside the vast number of star , there be empty space in the firmament for as many , nay for many more than as many yet : and then admire with astonishment the vast extent and amplitude of the heaven of heaven , which contain all these , and all that be above , and all that be below all these , within its circuit and circumference . second , it|be brightness and beauty . the brightness of the sun , the moon and the star do but imperfect represent the beauty and brightness of heaven as it|be describe rev. 21.23 . rev. 21.23 . and the city have no need of the sun , nor of the moon to shine in it , for the glory of God do lighten it , and the lamb be the light thereof , which exceed as much the light of the sun , as the sun light transcend that of a glimmer taper . the air of this country of heaven be continual pure , and clear , bright and splendid ;
